result.markdown:
--- title: "Simulation" subtitle: "Generate clean BOLD responses, noise, and matrix time series." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Clean Condition Signals `simulate_bold_signal()` creates condition-wise HRF-convolved responses. The first column of `mat` is time; the remaining columns are condition signals. ::: {#5cd5f672 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import fmrimod as fm sim = fm.simulate_bold_signal( ncond=3, amps=[1.0, 1.5, 2.0], tr=2.0, seed=10, ) signals = pd.DataFrame( sim["mat"], columns=["time", "cond_1", "cond_2", "cond_3"], ) signals.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ```{=html}
time cond_1 cond_2 cond_3
0 0.0 0.0 0.0 0.000000
1 2.0 0.0 0.0 0.000000
2 4.0 0.0 0.0 0.000398
3 6.0 0.0 0.0 1.052467
4 8.0 0.0 0.0 3.310880
``` ::: ::: ::: {#0fd0bb1f .cell fig-height='3.5' fig-width='7' execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} ax = signals.plot(x="time", y=["cond_1", "cond_2", "cond_3"]) ax.set_xlabel("Time (s)") ax.set_ylabel("Response") plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](simulation_files/figure-html/cell-3-output-1.png){width=1200 height=895} ::: ::: ## Noise Components `simulate_noise_vector()` combines temporal correlation, low-frequency drift, and optional physiological sinusoids. ::: {#bebd11e9 .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} n_timepoints = 200 tr = 2.0 time = np.arange(n_timepoints) * tr whiteish = fm.simulate_noise_vector( n_timepoints, tr=tr, ar=[], ma=[], drift_amplitude=0.0, physio=False, seed=1, ) structured = fm.simulate_noise_vector(n_timepoints, tr=tr, seed=1) pd.DataFrame({ "whiteish_sd": [whiteish.std()], "structured_sd": [structured.std()], }).round(3) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
whiteish_sd structured_sd
0 0.924 1.708
``` ::: ::: ::: {#b601f2ac .cell fig-height='3.5' fig-width='7' execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} noise_df = pd.DataFrame({ "time": time, "whiteish": whiteish, "structured": structured, }) ax = noise_df.plot(x="time", y=["whiteish", "structured"], alpha=0.85) ax.set_xlabel("Time (s)") ax.set_ylabel("Noise") plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](simulation_files/figure-html/cell-5-output-1.png){width=1223 height=895} ::: ::: ## Signal Plus Noise `simulate_simple_dataset()` returns clean and noisy matrices for small method checks. ::: {#ffc52e05 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} dataset = fm.simulate_simple_dataset(ncond=3, tr=2.0, snr=0.5, seed=2) dataset["clean"].shape, dataset["noisy"].shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` ((157, 4), (157, 4)) ``` ::: ::: ## Matrix Time Series `simulate_fmri_matrix()` varies event amplitude and duration across columns, mirroring the R vignette's column-specific simulation examples. ::: {#b28dd802 .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} matrix_sim = fm.simulate_fmri_matrix( n=5, total_time=120, TR=2.0, n_events=8, durations=0.5, amplitude_sd=0.2, duration_sd=0.1, noise_type="ar1", random_seed=3, ) ts = matrix_sim["time_series"].get_all_data() ts.shape, matrix_sim["ampmat"].shape, matrix_sim["durmat"].shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ``` ((60, 5), (8, 5), (8, 5)) ``` ::: ::: ## Next - [Benchmark datasets](benchmark-datasets.qmd) - [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
